original_abstract: Despite the rapid proliferation of carbon dots (CDs) in materials science, the fundamental mechanisms governing their formation remain poorly understood. This study elucidates the synthesis of CDs via a one-step hydrothermal treatment of 4-aminoantipyrine. By systematically investigating the evolution of the material across varying reaction intervals, we provide a detailed schematic of the polymerization and carbonization pathways. This graphical representation offers critical insights into the sequential stages of CD development, clarifying the chemical transitions essential to their formation.
humanized_abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) have been extensively studied in the field of materials science in recent years. However, there is only limited understanding of the mechanism of their formation. Herein, we reported the fabrication of CDs via the one-step hydrothermal treatment of 4-aminoantipyrine. Moreover, through optimizing reaction time, the evolvement process of the as-obtained material was elucidated thoroughly and depicted into a schematic model for polymerization and carbonization process during the evolution of CDs.
